commit.message:
🎉 Initial release: .NET MAUI backend for Windows Forms

A complete .NET MAUI backend powered by System.Windows.Forms — run your
MAUI apps natively on Windows with real WinForms controls.

## What's included

### Core Backend (46 handlers)
- All standard controls: Label, Button, Entry, Editor, Image, Switch,
  CheckBox, RadioButton, Slider, Stepper, ProgressBar, ActivityIndicator,
  Picker, DatePicker, TimePicker, SearchBar, ImageButton, BoxView
- All layouts: Grid (with ColSpan/RowSpan), VerticalStackLayout,
  HorizontalStackLayout, FlexLayout, AbsoluteLayout, ScrollView,
  ContentView, Border
- All pages: ContentPage, NavigationPage, TabbedPage, FlyoutPage, Shell
  (flyout sidebar + tab bars + route navigation)
- Collections: CollectionView, ListView, CarouselView, TableView,
  IndicatorView, SwipeView, RefreshView
- Graphics: GraphicsView + all shapes (Rectangle, Ellipse, Line, Path,
  Polygon, Polyline) via GDI+
- WebView: Full WebView2 integration (URL, HTML, JavaScript, navigation)
- Gestures: Tap, Pan, Swipe, Pointer (hover), Drag & Drop — with child
  control event bubbling and reflection-based SendTapped/SendPointerEntered

### Blazor Hybrid
- BlazorWebViewHandler wrapping Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.WindowsForms
- Full JS interop, static asset serving, component parameters

### Essentials (15 services)
- AppInfo, Browser, Clipboard, Connectivity, DeviceDisplay, DeviceInfo,
  Email, FilePicker, FileSystem, Launcher, Preferences (JSON),
  Screenshot, SecureStorage (DPAPI), VersionTracking

### DevFlow Agent Integration
- Screenshots via PrintWindow, visual tree walking, CDP bridge for Blazor
- Auto-start via Application.Idle, 3-level service resolution fallback
- maui-labs submodule at external/maui-labs for Agent.Core dependency

### Sample Apps
- ControlGallery: 23-page Shell app covering every control
- Simple Sample: basic counter app
- Blazor Hybrid Sample: Home/Counter/Weather pages

### Developer Experience
- dotnet new templates: maui-winforms and maui-winforms-blazor
- CI/CD via GitHub Actions (build + pack + NuGet push on release)
- NuGet packages with SourceLink, symbol packages (.snupkg), full metadata
- TreatWarningsAsErrors enabled, 0 warnings across entire solution
- LF line endings enforced via .gitattributes
- BACKEND_IMPLEMENTATION_CHECKLIST.md with detailed per-feature status

### Build
  dotnet build platforms/Windows.WinForms/Windows.WinForms.slnx
  # 0 warnings, 0 errors

### Audit
  23/23 pages pass, 200+ control interactions, zero crashes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor(logics): remove flat schema, align deploy eos with Ansible standard model (#72)

* feat(inventory): add multi-group flat inventory schema with credential inheritance

Introduce a second inventory format (globals/groups/hosts) auto-detected
alongside the existing Ansible-style schema. Hosts can belong to multiple
groups; connection parameters (credentials, tls_verify, kind, address) are
resolved with deterministic priority: host > first-matching group > globals,
with field-by-field fallback.

## New components

- `ConnectionInventoryLoader`: schema auto-detection, flat + ansible parsing,
  credential resolution. Raises `InvalidInventoryError` on ambiguous schema
  (flat keys mixed with non-flat top-level keys).
- `ConnectionInventory` / `ResolvedHostConnection` / `ResolvedCredentials`:
  immutable value-object models for resolved connection data. Credentials
  are masked in all display paths.

## Credential key aliases supported

- `credentials.username` / `credentials.password`
- `ansible_user` / `ansible_password`
- `address` / `ansible_host`

## Commands updated

- `deploy eos`: uses ConnectionInventoryLoader; hosts without kind=arista_eos
  silently skipped; per-host tls_verify overrides global verify_ssl flag.
- `info`: displays resolved connection table (credentials masked) for flat
  schema; JSON/YAML output includes schema field.
- `validate`: detects flat schema and validates structure + resolution.

## Backward compatibility

Ansible-style inventories are unchanged. The deployer `verify_ssl` parameter
becomes Optional[bool]; None means inherit per-host setting.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: add spec/ to .gitignore

Planning docs live in GitHub Issues, not in the repo.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(logics): reduce cyclomatic complexity in inventory loader and deployer

Extract private helper methods to bring all methods below flake8 C901
threshold (max complexity 10):

- ConnectionInventoryLoader._parse_flat_inventory: extract _resolve_flat_host
- ConnectionInventoryLoader._extract_ansible_hosts_recursive: extract
  _resolve_ansible_host and _recurse_ansible_children
- Deployer._build_targets: extract _host_passes_filter,
  _assert_flat_host_complete, and _build_deployment_target

Also: fix E501 (line > 120 chars) in _resolve_tls_from_levels and
add ResolvedHostConnection to TYPE_CHECKING imports in deployer.

Fix 5 critical bugs in MailMerge-Pro taskpane.js

Bug 1: Large attachments (>3MB) now use Graph upload sessions instead of
failing silently. Added uploadLargeAttachment() helper with chunked upload.

Bug 2: Many-to-one merge now collects and dedupes attachments from ALL
rows in the group, not just the first row.

Bug 3: Merge field values are HTML-escaped when building HTML email bodies
and previews, preventing XSS injection from spreadsheet data. Subject lines
and plain text bodies remain unescaped.

Bug 4: All localStorage.setItem() calls replaced with safeLocalStorageSet()
wrapper that catches QuotaExceededError and shows a user-friendly warning
instead of crashing.

Bug 5: Data preview table now uses pagination (50 rows per page) with
Previous/Next controls. Search filters the data model instead of scanning
DOM nodes, preventing UI freezes on large spreadsheets.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: OneNote section reader — step 1 for RAG indexing (#76)

* feat: add OneNote section reader for RAG content extraction

Extract text from .one (MS-ONESTORE) binary files without requiring
OneNote to be installed or a native binary parser.

- New service: src/services/onenote-reader.ts
  - Hybrid UTF-16BE + ASCII string extraction
  - 'PageTitle' sentinel-based page boundary detection
  - Returns OneNoteSection with per-page titles, dates, and content,
    plus a combined textContent blob for whole-section RAG indexing
  - Noise filter removes font names, GUIDs, XML fragments, and
    OneNote property keys from extracted text

- New IPC handler: onedrive:read-onenote-file
  - Accepts a full file path, validates .one extension
  - Returns { ok, section } or { ok: false, error }

- Types: OneNotePageContent and OneNoteSectionContent added to
  plugins/types.ts; onedriveReadOneNoteFile added to JarvisApi

- Preload: onedriveReadOneNoteFile exposed via contextBridge

- Tests: 19 new tests using fixture .one files from the installed
  @joplin/onenote-converter package (no binary blobs committed)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: add OneNote folder integration tests

Proves the full group→OneDrive discovery→file scan→content extraction
pipeline using the @joplin/onenote-converter fixture files as a stand-in
OneDrive root.

- Sets up an in-memory DB group named to match a fixture subdirectory
- Runs discoverCustomerFolderForGroup, scanFilesForFolder, listFilesForFolder
- Reads every .one file found via readOneNoteSection
- Logs a formatted section/page summary (visible with --reporter=verbose)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add OneNote section viewer panel in Groups UI

When a OneDrive folder has .one files, each file now shows a 📖 button
in the expanded file list. Clicking it opens a OneNoteSectionPanel to
the right of GroupsPanel showing the extracted content.

- OneNoteSectionPanel: reads the file via onedriveReadOneNoteFile IPC,
  shows section name + page count in the header, then a card per page
  with title (heading), date, and body text (expandable past 320 chars)
- GroupsPanel: accepts onOpenOneNote callback, renders 📓 icon + 📖 button
  for .one files; constructs full path from folderPath + relativePath
- index.tsx: wires up oneNoteFilePath state, passes onOpenOneNote prop,
  renders OneNoteSectionPanel in groups-layout; clears on panel close
- onboarding.css: adds .onenote-section-panel (420px, max-height 80vh)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: read and open .url Internet Shortcut files in file list

OneNote notebooks hosted on SharePoint are represented locally as .url
shortcut files instead of .one files. This adds first-class support:

- New service src/services/url-shortcut.ts: parses the INI-format .url
  file and returns { url, isOneNote, isSharePoint }
- New IPC handler onedrive:read-url-shortcut: reads .url file -> URL
- New IPC handler shell:open-url: opens a validated https:/onenote: URL
  in the system browser/OneNote app
- GroupsPanel: pre-loads .url info when a folder expands; shows
  notebook+globe icon for OneNote links, link icon for other URLs,
  SharePoint badge, and an Open button that launches the URL

Content extraction requires Microsoft Graph API (notebook lives on
SharePoint, not locally synced). The open button is the practical
workaround until Graph API auth is added.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use URL hostname parsing instead of substring check for SharePoint

CodeQL flagged incomplete URL substring sanitization: using .includes()
allows spoofing via paths or subdomains (e.g. evil.com/sharepoint.com).

Replace with proper URL parsing: check parsed.hostname equals or ends
with '.sharepoint.com' to safely identify SharePoint-hosted links.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] fix: Markdown.ToMd preserves DirectLink and DirectImage titles (#1150)

* fix: Markdown.ToMd preserves DirectLink and DirectImage titles

When serialising a document with ToMd, link and image titles were
silently dropped.  A Markdown fragment such as

    [text](url "My Title")

would round-trip as

    [text](url)

because the `DirectLink` / `DirectImage` formatSpan cases matched the
title field with a wildcard.

Fix: give DirectLink and DirectImage their own branches so the optional
title can be appended in CommonMark form (  \"title\").

Three new round-trip tests cover:
  - DirectLink with title
  - DirectLink without title (unchanged behaviour)
  - DirectImage with title

All 284 Markdown tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] perf/refactor: convert ILFieldDefs/ILEventDefs/ILPropertyDefs to concrete classes with lazy name-lookup caches (#502)

🤖 *This PR was created by [Repo
Assist](https://github.com/githubnext/agentics/blob/main/docs/repo-assist.md),
an automated AI assistant.*

## Summary

This PR converts `ILFieldDefs`, `ILEventDefs`, and `ILPropertyDefs` from
abstract interfaces to concrete classes, mirroring the existing
`ILMethodDefs` pattern. It adds lazy O(1) name-lookup caches throughout
and makes entries truly lazy (computed at most once per type).

### Background

Previously these three types were abstract interfaces:

```fsharp
// Before — computed on every .Entries access; linear O(n) name lookups everywhere
type ILFieldDefs =
    abstract Entries: ILFieldDef[]
```

Every name-based lookup (`GetField`, `GetPropertyImpl`, `GetEvent`,
`GetEnumUnderlyingType`) called `Array.tryFind` — an O(n) linear scan.
Additionally, `seekReadEvents` and `seekReadProperties` recomputed the
entries array on every `.Entries` access (the binary reader range scan
ran again each time).

### Changes

**Concrete classes with lazy dict caches** — mirroring `ILMethodDefs`:

```fsharp
type ILFieldDefs(larr: Lazy<ILFieldDef[]>) =
    let lmap = lazy (
        let d = Dictionary<string, ILFieldDef>()
        for f in larr.Force() do d.[f.Name] <- f
        d)
    member __.Entries = larr.Force()
    member __.TryFindByName nm =
        let scc, v = lmap.Value.TryGetValue(nm) in if scc then Some v else None
```

Similarly for `ILEventDefs` and `ILPropertyDefs`.

**Entries are now truly lazy** — `seekReadEvents`/`seekReadProperties`
previously re-ran the range scan on every `.Entries` call. Now the lazy
constructor caches after first access.

**O(1) lookups in `TypeSymbol` and `TargetTypeDefinition`**:
- `TypeSymbol.GetField/GetPropertyImpl/GetEvent` (generic
instantiations): now use `TryFindByName` instead of `Array.tryFind`
- `TargetTypeDefinition.GetField/GetPropertyImpl/GetEvent`: new lazy
`fieldDefsMap`/`propDefsMap`/`eventDefsMap` dictionaries over the
wrapped reflection objects
- `TargetTypeDefinition.GetEnumUnderlyingType`: uses `TryFindByName` for
the `"value__"` field

**All creation sites updated**: empty constants,
`seekReadFields`/`seekReadEvents`/`seekReadProperties`, and
`ProvidedTypeBuilder`.

### Trade-offs

- Slightly higher memory use per loaded `ILTypeDef` (dictionary
overhead), but only allocated on first name lookup
- No API surface changes — `Entries` still works identically;
`TryFindByName` is a new additive method

### Relation to issue #500

This PR implements the code improvements from the blocked issue #500
(which was blocked by the inclusion of `.github/dependabot.yml`). This
PR contains only the code changes, without the Dependabot config.

## Test Status

tests/FSharp.TypeProviders.SDK.Tests.fsproj -c Release --framework
net8.0`)

Build: `dotnet build src/FSharp.TypeProviders.SDK.fsproj -c Release` —
succeeded, 0 warnings, 0 errors.

commit.message:
fix: SSRF via /__sd__/, user-replace corrupting /__sd__/ host, HEAD+gzip EOF

Three bug fixes:

1. SSRF via /__sd__/ (CRITICAL)
   In the director, validate that the host extracted from /__sd__/<host>/
   is actually a subdomain of rootDomain before routing to it. Without
   this check any client could use /__sd__/evil.com/ to proxy arbitrary
   external hosts. Invalid hosts are logged and re-routed to the main target.

2. User replacements corrupting /__sd__/ host segment
   withExternalURLsProtected now shields /__sd__/<host> segments using
   subdomainPathRe before applying user alias replacements. This prevents
   e.g. -replace ynet:news from turning /__sd__/www.ynet.co.il/ into
   /__sd__/www.news.co.il/ and breaking subdomain routing.

3. HEAD / 204 / 304 responses triggering false 'failed to decode gzip' error
   Upstreams may send Content-Encoding: gzip on HEAD responses even though
   there is no body (correct per HTTP spec). Added an early return for
   no-body responses (HEAD, 204, 304) before attempting gzip.NewReader.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] fix: ProvidedTypeDefinition.Logger broken (new ref each call); add 5 tests for warning + enum round-trip (#501)

🤖 *This PR was created by [Repo
Assist](https://github.com/githubnext/agentics/blob/main/docs/repo-assist.md),
an automated AI assistant.*

## Summary

This PR bundles a **bug fix** and **new tests** (Task 9: Testing
Improvements).

### Bug fix: `ProvidedTypeDefinition.Logger` was silently broken

The static `Logger` member was implemented as a computed property:

```fsharp
// Before — creates a fresh ref cell on every access; impossible to set
static member Logger: (string -> unit) option ref = ref None
```

Every read of `ProvidedTypeDefinition.Logger` returned a *different*
`ref` object, so calling `ProvidedTypeDefinition.Logger := Some f` would
write into a temporary cell that is immediately discarded. The Logger
was effectively inoperative: the "all static parameters optional"
warning (added in 8.3.0 / PR #428) could never fire via the Logger, and
type-creation trace messages were silently dropped.

**Fix**: introduces a `static let loggerRef` field and makes `Logger`
return it:

```fsharp
static let loggerRef: (string -> unit) option ref = ref None
...
static member Logger: (string -> unit) option ref = loggerRef
```

### New tests (5 tests, 131 total)

**`BasicErasedProvisionTests.fs` — 3 new tests for the all-optional
warning:**
- `DefineStaticParameters warns when all static parameters have
defaults` — regression for the warning path (which was previously
unreachable due to the Logger bug)
- `DefineStaticParameters does not warn when at least one parameter has
no default`
- `DefineStaticParameters does not warn when there are no static
parameters`

**`GenerativeEnumsProvisionTests.fs` — 2 new tests for non-Int32 enum
round-trip via target context:**
- `Byte enum underlying type is correct when read via target context
(ReadRelatedAssembly)` — exercises
`TargetTypeDefinition.GetEnumUnderlyingType()` for a `byte`-backed enum
- `Int64 enum underlying type is correct when read via target context
(ReadRelatedAssembly)` — same for `int64`-backed enums

These complement the existing runtime-path enum tests (`Assembly.Load`)
by covering the design-time IL-reader path, which was fixed in PR
#470/#475.

## Trade-offs

- The Logger ref is now module-level shared state. Tests that manipulate
it should save/restore the previous value (all three new tests do this
via `try/finally`).

## Test Status

✅ All 131 tests pass (`dotnet test
tests/FSharp.TypeProviders.SDK.Tests.fsproj -c Release`)

v0.2.0: Best-in-class tool integrations + complete UI/UX overhaul

Tool Integrations (7 open-source tools):
- ClamAV: Deep malware scanning with millions of signatures
- osquery: SQL-powered system auditing (launch items, ports, extensions, firewall)
- mactop: Apple Silicon metrics (CPU/GPU, temperatures, power, per-core)
- mas: Mac App Store CLI for App Store app updates
- fclones: High-performance duplicate file detection
- czkawka: Similar images, videos, music, and temp file detection
- gdu: Fast disk usage analysis for Space Lens visualization

New Features:
- Space Lens module: Visual disk usage map with expandable directory tree
- Similar Images/Videos/Temp Files tabs in Declutter (via czkawka)
- Mac App Store updates in Update module (via mas)
- System audit section in Protect (via osquery)
- GPU/temperature/power metrics in Speed (via mactop)
- Parallel Smart Care scanning with TaskGroup + cancel button
- Tappable ModuleCards navigate to module details

UX Improvements:
- Confirmation dialogs for all destructive actions
- Error banners replacing silent failures
- Persistent VM state across navigation (shared via AppState)
- ProgressRing with color thresholds (green/yellow/red)
- Per-item selection in Clean module with disclosure triangles
- Interactive duplicate keep selection
- Reveal in Finder context menus throughout
- Auto-refresh toggle for Speed gauges (5s interval)
- Sort options for Large Files (size/date)
- Dependency banners for all optional tools with one-click install

Design System:
- Added warning (amber) and info (blue-grey) semantic colors
- Added animation tokens (standard/slow/spring)
- Unified ProgressRing component replaces 3 duplicate implementations
